visual_features.overall_style: Clean, corporate e‑commerce style with strong brand colouring (yellow + blue), flat design, and minimal decorative elements. Emphasis is on clarity and straightforward browsing rather than high-end editorial aesthetics.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is created by a very bold coloured header (black top bar + yellow main bar), then a large centered page title ('Sea water membrane'), followed by a horizontal category filter and then product cards. Strong contrast (yellow/black, blue/white, green/white) drives attention to the header, navigation, and chat CTA. The floating chat widget and WhatsApp button act as secondary attention grabbers due to their saturated colours and elevated placement.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: The content area around the page title and category filter is extremely spacious with large vertical whitespace, giving the main heading strong prominence but making the page feel somewhat sparse above the product grid. The header and search area are more compact but still maintain adequate padding. Product area (partially visible) appears to use card spacing with margins around each card.
unique_elements: Full-width yellow main header bar contrasting with a thin black utility bar above it
Prominent left-aligned 'Browse Categories' control that visually reads like a primary navigation button
Floating chat widget with branded header and pill-shaped green 'Start chat' CTA overlaying the main content
Floating circular WhatsApp action button anchored at the bottom-right
Category filter strip just beneath the main title with ‘All’ highlighted in red as the active tab

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid typical of WooCommerce/WordPress themes, with content centered in a fixed-width container inside a full-bleed header.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Standard e-commerce layout with full-width header and top navigation, followed by a full-width search/category bar, then a central page title and horizontal category filter, leading into a multi-column product grid. Overlayed right-side chat panel and bottom-right floating action button.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: From top to bottom: (1) Thin black utility bar with brand name, (2) Thick yellow main header with logo and primary navigation + account/cart icons, (3) Light-grey/white bar containing 'Browse Categories' button, search field, and category dropdown, (4) Breadcrumb row, (5) Large centered page title, (6) Horizontal category filter tabs, (7) Product list/grid area. Chat widget sits on top of sections anchored to the right.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall left-aligned content within a centered container. Navigation links are horizontally aligned in the header. The main title is center-aligned. Breadcrumbs and filters are left-aligned. Buttons and controls within bars are aligned on a consistent baseline. Floating elements (chat, WhatsApp) are aligned to the right edge of the viewport.
responsive_hints: Large touch-friendly controls (Browse Categories, search bar, floating chat and WhatsApp buttons) suggest mobile responsiveness.
Horizontal nav with relatively few items implies that, on smaller screens, navigation will likely collapse into a hamburger or stacked menu.
The use of a central column and roomy margins indicates that on mobile the content can stack vertically without major redesign.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: black utility bar → yellow main header with logo + nav → horizontal bar with left 'Browse Categories' button and central search input → breadcrumb row → spacious gap → centered H1 ('Sea water membrane') → another vertical gap → a single-row horizontal category filter (text links for All, Auto Multiport Valve, Bag Filter Assembly, etc.) → below that, a left-aligned multi-column product grid (only the first product card is partially visible). On top of the main content, a right-aligned chat panel overlays the page, with a floating WhatsApp icon anchored at the bottom-right, both independent of the content flow.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header band (black + yellow + search bar area) visually occupies roughly the top 20–25% of the viewport. The midsection (breadcrumb + large whitespace + title + category filter) takes another ~20–25% and feels very open. The lower half is dedicated to product content but is only partially visible in the screenshot. Horizontal space: header elements span the full width, but central content is constrained in a main column, giving generous left/right margins. Chat widget and WhatsApp FAB occupy a small but visually significant chunk of the right-side area due to their contrasting colour blocks.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately low density near the top and mid-page: elements are spread out with generous vertical spacing and minimal simultaneous information, conveying clarity but leaving empty areas. Product listing section appears denser, with product images and labels closer together. Overall, the layout feels clean and breathable rather than information-packed.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Typical Z-pattern on desktop: eye starts at the top-left logo/brand name on the black bar, continues right across the yellow navigation bar and account/cart icons, then drops diagonally to the left side search area/breadcrumb row, moves right across the search and category dropdown, then down to the large centered page title, continues left-to-right across the horizontal category filter, and finally scans downward into the product grid. The floating blue chat widget and green WhatsApp button pull the eye to the right edge as secondary focal points.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Two main button styles: (1) Flat rectangular buttons with soft rounded corners (~4px radius), solid fill (e.g., white within the 'Browse Categories' control) and a light grey border. Text is centered, in a medium weight sans-serif, with a black or dark-grey colour. Icon (hamburger) sits to the left inside the same button with adequate padding. (2) Pill-shaped CTA buttons, as seen in the chat widget ('Start chat'): high corner radius (~999px), solid green fill (#28A745-ish) with white bold text, no visible border, and probably a subtle hover state. Overall flat design without heavy shadows.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Primary actions likely implemented as <button> elements or anchor tags styled as buttons (<a class="btn btn-primary">). Internal content structured as an inline-flex wrapper with optional icon span + text span for alignment. For the header 'Browse Categories' button: wrapper div containing an icon element (<i> or svg) followed by a text node.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Product cards use a white background with clear separation from the page background. A soft drop shadow or subtle border is implied by the way the product image is framed, though not fully visible. The card shows a product image with perspective/angle, possibly with a small black sale badge on the top-left reading 'SALE 5%'. Corners appear slightly rounded (~4px). Inner padding is moderate (16–24px), with text and price likely below the image in a stacked arrangement.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical e-commerce card structure: <article> or <li class="product-card"> with a container for the product image (<figure> or <div class="product-image">) and an absolutely positioned badge for discount. Below, a content area <div class="product-info"> holding product name (heading), price, and CTA (e.g., Add to Cart button). Cards are placed inside a responsive grid (flexbox or CSS grid) with consistent gutters.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Search bar uses a large, full-width input with light grey background (#FAFAFA–#F5F5F5) and likely a subtle border. Corners are slightly rounded, consistent with button styling. Placeholder text ('Search for items') is light grey. The category dropdown on the right mirrors the same visual language: white/very light-grey fill, subtle border, down-arrow icon. Labels are not visually present; the placeholder and contextual positioning act as affordances. Overall form density is low and visually uncluttered.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Search form likely implemented as a <form> with an <input type="search"> plus an embedded button/icon (magnifying glass) inside or adjacent. Inputs are stacked horizontally inside a flex container. Category selector is a <select> element styled or replaced by a custom dropdown component within the same flex row. On mobile, these likely stack vertically.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal text menu on a yellow background. Items ('Home', 'Products', 'Our Blog', 'Contact Us') are spaced evenly with generous horizontal padding and are set in a medium-weight sans serif. Text is black for maximum contrast. Active state may be indicated by bold text, different colour, or underline (not clearly visible in screenshot). To the right are icon-based actions (account, wishlist with red circular notification badge, cart). The top utility bar is a narrow black strip with small white text for the brand name.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Two list patterns are visible: (1) Breadcrumb list: inline items separated by a '>' symbol, small, grey text following a subtle hierarchy (home > products > tag). (2) Category filter tabs: horizontally aligned text-only tabs; inactive ones are plain dark text, while the active 'All' category is red, visually indicating current filter. No heavy tab borders, mostly text-based styling with small margins between items. Product listing itself is a grid of cards rather than a traditional list.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Breadcrumbs structured as
  1. ...
    2. ...
, with CSS providing inline display and separators via ::before pseudo-elements. Category filter uses a flexbox row of

heading.visual_style: Bold geometric or neo-grotesque sans-serif with a clean, modern appearance and relatively high x-height. Simple forms, uniform stroke widths, suited for clear headings like 'Sea water membrane'.

body.visual_style: Neutral, readable sans-serif with humanist touches, good for product descriptions and navigation text. The current design emphasizes legibility over personality.

spacing_system.component_padding: Most interactive components (buttons, inputs, chat bubble) appear to use 8–16px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, with inner spacing aligned to the 8px grid.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (header → search bar → breadcrumb → title → filter → product grid) is roughly 24–48px, creating clear separation and a calm layout.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely underline or change colour on hover (e.g., deeper black or blue) to indicate interactivity.
Buttons such as 'Start chat' probably darken slightly or add a subtle shadow on hover, with a cursor change to pointer.
Category filter tabs likely change to red and/or use a bottom border when active/hovered.
Floating chat widget can be closed via the 'X' icon; opening/closing is likely animated with a slide or fade transition from the right.
WhatsApp floating button probably has a hover state (slight scale-up or shadow) and opens WhatsApp Web or mobile app on click.
Subtle focus outlines or glow are recommended for accessibility on form fields and buttons, using the brand blue as focus colour.
